Anthony Nnaji

Robotics  ·  Control Systems  ·  Embedded Systems

Mechatronics Engineering graduate with a big smile, experience in instrumentation, calibration, mechanical design, and embedded system development. Skilled in control systems design, robotic simulation, and engineering analysis, I am eager to apply my technical foundation professionally and contribute to innovative teams. Excited to take on new challenges and bring value wherever I go. Feel free to reach out.

Work

Projects

More →
Quadruped Robot Development

Built an open-source quadruped robot using Arduino IDE, integrating motors, sensors, and drivers for coordinated leg motion and locomotion experiments.

Arduino IDEEmbedded SystemsMotor ControlSensor Integration
More →
6-DOF Robotic Manipulator

Built a complete 6-DOF robotic arm from mechanical assembly to a custom Arduino-based control system, achieving smooth servo interpolation across all six joints.

ArduinoMechanical DesignServo Control6-DOF Kinematics
More →
Trajectory Tracking — Two-Link Manipulator

Implemented Boundary-Layer SMC assisted PID on a nonlinear robotic manipulator in MATLAB/Simulink, assessing robustness under disturbances and parameter uncertainty.

MATLAB / SimulinkSMC + PIDNonlinear Control
More →
Wearable Knee Exoskeleton Joint Control

Developed a MATLAB/Simulink simulation comparing classical PID against a hybrid PID-SMC strategy for a knee exoskeleton, evaluating tracking accuracy and disturbance rejection.

MATLAB / SimulinkPID vs SMCWearable Robotics
More →
Optimal Computed Torque Control via Genetic Algorithm

Modeled a nonlinear 3-DOF manipulator and applied Genetic Algorithm optimization to tune computed torque PD gains, improving trajectory tracking over manual baselines.

MATLAB / SimulinkGenetic AlgorithmComputed Torque
